clinical_history: ECG from an elderly man. What is the rapid rhythm disturbance?
diagnosis: The ECG shows atrial fibrillation (AF) with a very rapid ventricular response. The average resting heart rate (over 10 sec of data) is 120/min, with instantaneous heart rates ranging from about 80-170/min. There is an intraventricular conduction delay (QRS duration about 130 ms). Left axis deviation is present, consistent with left anterior fascicular block. Slow R wave progression and non-specific ST-T changes are present. Lead aVL shows a prominent but non-diagnostic Q wave.  The patient had severe ischemic cardiomyopathy, with prior extensive anterior wall infarction. The constellation of ECG abnormalities is consistent with that diagnosis.
